Dangerous occurrences are certain incidents with a high potential to cause death or serious injury such as the collapse of scaffolding, failure of any load bearing part of any lifting equipment, malfunction of breathing apparatus, electrical short circuit or overload causing a fire or explosion, accidental release of any substance which may damage health, plant or equipment coming into contact with overhead power lines, etc. United Kingdom Northern Ireland
Please enter the total number of reportable dangerous occurrences for the given year:

Work-related accidents must be reported where they result in an employee (or self-employed person) being away from work, or unable to do their normal work duties, for more than 7 consecutive days as the result of their injury. Where the worker’s injury or condition does not become apparent until some time after the accident, it must be reported as soon as it has prevented them from doing their normal work duties for more than 7 consecutive days. This 7-day period does not include the day of the accident, but does include weekends and rest days. The report must be made within 15 days of the accident. Some situations will include days when the injured person would not normally have been expected to work. You must take those days into account when deciding whether they were unable to do their normal duties for ‘more than 7 consecutive days’.

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

Accidents must be recorded where they result in a worker being away from work, or unable to do their normal work duties, for more than 3 consecutive days. As an employer, you don’t need to report this type of accident – recording it in your accident book (under social security law) will be enough.
